opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My testicle is the size of a tennis ball, it feels incredibly heavy, and I am terrified it is advanced cancer. It's blown up and it's dragging and I panicked tonight after I was lifting some boxes.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The swelling has felt huge and heavy all day and after I did some heavy lifting and a lot of standing tonight I got really scared it's cancer or something that needs an emergency.

symptom_narrative.onset: it's come on slowly over about six months, not all of a sudden
symptom_narrative.duration: about six months of gradual swelling, worse-feeling tonight
symptom_narrative.character: a big, heavy, swollen bag around the testicle with a dragging feeling; it doesn't actually hurt, it's just heavy and uncomfortable
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it's not painful but it feels enormous and the weight of it is really bothering me and frightening me
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the right side of my scrotum, all around the testicle on that side
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there all the time, feels heavier after standing and lifting
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: it feels a bit less draggy when I sit or lie down and take the weight off
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing for a long time and heavy lifting make the dragging feeling worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no pain, no burning when I pee, no fevers, I haven't hurt it. My tummy is fine, I'm not vomiting and my bowels are normal.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before — it's just slowly got bigger over the last few months
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm walking a bit differently to keep the weight off it, and it's on my mind constantly

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: very anxious tonight and frightened it is cancer; otherwise well, independent, no particular social risk

objective_data_offered.value: the right side of my scrotum is hugely swollen, about the size of a tennis ball, but the skin looks a normal colour — it's not red or purple and it doesn't feel hot
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-shown on video; colour and size impression only, no examination performed

objective_data_offered.value: it honestly doesn't hurt when I touch it or press it, it's just heavy
about 82 kg on my home scales
I don't feel feverish and I haven't had any chills
